yaml.title: Dashboard
yaml.subtitle: Used car pricing data visualization and business interpretation

markdown.markdown:
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)

used_cars <- tibble::tribble(
  ~brand, ~model, ~year, ~price, ~mileage, ~brand_reputation,
  "Toyota", "Camry", 2021, 22995, 42000, 5,
  "Toyota", "Corolla", 2020, 18995, 55000, 5,
  "Toyota", "RAV4", 2022, 28995, 36000, 5,
  "Honda", "Civic", 2021, 21995, 39000, 5,
  "Honda", "Accord", 2020, 23995, 47000, 5,
  "Honda", "CR-V", 2022, 29995, 34000, 5,
  "Ford", "Fusion", 2019, 15995, 71000, 3,
  "Ford", "Escape", 2021, 21495, 52000, 3,
  "Ford", "Explorer", 2020, 26995, 61000, 3,
  "Chevrolet", "Malibu", 2019, 15495, 76000, 3,
  "Chevrolet", "Equinox", 2021, 20495, 58000, 3,
  "Chevrolet", "Traverse", 2020, 25995, 63000, 3,
  "Nissan", "Altima", 2020, 17995, 69000, 4,
  "Nissan", "Sentra", 2021, 16995, 48000, 4,
  "Nissan", "Rogue", 2022, 23995, 41000, 4
) |>
  mutate(
    vehicle_age = 2026 - year,
    price_thousands = price / 1000
  )
```


This dashboard visualizes selected used car pricing info by comparing price, mileage, age, and brand. The dashboard supports the research question of which factors appear connected to used vehicle value.

---

## Key Metrics Summary

```{r}
summary_table <- used_cars |>
  summarise(
    `Average Price` = dollar(mean(price)),
    `Average Mileage` = comma(round(mean(mileage), 0)),
    `Average Vehicle Age` = round(mean(vehicle_age), 1),
    `Highest Price` = dollar(max(price)),
    `Lowest Price` = dollar(min(price))
  )

knitr::kable(summary_table)
```

---

## Average Price by Brand

```{r}
used_cars |>
  group_by(brand) |>
  summarise(avg_price = mean(price), .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(brand, avg_price), y = avg_price)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Used Car Price by Brand",
    x = "Brand",
    y = "Average Price",
    caption = "Source: Sample used car listing dataset"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

---

## Mileage Compared to Price

```{r}
used_cars |>
  ggplot(aes(x = mileage, y = price)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = comma_format()) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Relationship Between Mileage and Price",
    x = "Mileage",
    y = "Price",
    caption = "Source: Sample used car listing dataset"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

---

## Vehicle Age Compared to Price

```{r}
used_cars |>
  ggplot(aes(x = vehicle_age, y = price)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Relationship Between Vehicle Age and Price",
    x = "Vehicle Age",
    y = "Price",
    caption = "Source: Sample used car listing dataset"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

---

## Brand Reputation and Average Price

```{r}
used_cars |>
  group_by(brand_reputation) |>
  summarise(avg_price = mean(price), .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(brand_reputation), y = avg_price)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Price by Brand Reputation Score",
    x = "Brand Reputation Score",
    y = "Average Price",
    caption = "Source: Sample used car listing dataset"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

---

## Price Distribution

```{r}
used_cars |>
  ggplot(aes(x = price)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 6) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Used Car Prices",
    x = "Price",
    y = "Number of Vehicles",
    caption = "Source: Sample used car listing dataset"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

---

## Business Interpretation

Overall, this dashboard suggests that used car pricing is influenced by make and mileage. For buyers, this information can help with determining whether a car is priced fairly.

---

## Skills Demonstrated

- Data visualization
- Market research
- Business analytics
- Data interpretation
- Consumer behavior analysis
- Quarto website development
